policy_statement: By 2030, achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings, reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings, and lower all natural gas-related emissions by 30%.
verbatim_text:
By 2030, achieve net-zero carbon* for all new buildings and reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings while lowering all natural gas-related emissions by 30%.

* For this goal, net-zero carbon implies operational carbon, which refers to the CO 2 emitted from operations, such as lighting and heating, during the in-use phase of a building.
mechanism_description: Stronger building emissions and energy performance requirements for new and existing buildings reduce operational energy use and associated fossil fuel emissions.

policy_statement: Pursue a comprehensive energy poverty mitigation strategy through partnerships and programs so building repair, conservation, and renewable energy benefits flow to low-income communities and communities of color.
parent_statement: By 2030, achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings, reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings, and lower all natural gas-related emissions by 30%.
verbatim_text: Pursue a comprehensive energy poverty mitigation strategy by partnering with trusted community organizations, affordable housing developers, and schools in equitable outreach and program development. Programs should focus on energy burden reduction, improved air quality, neighborhood resilience during extreme weather, and increased passive survivability of buildings. This will ensure that the benefits of repair, energy conservation, and renewable energy incentives and programs flow to low-income communities and communities of color.
mechanism_description: Partnership-based programs deliver repairs, efficiency improvements, and renewable energy support to underserved households, reducing energy burden while lowering building-related emissions and improving resilience.

policy_statement: Enhance resident and building owner understanding of energy savings and climate impacts through outreach, culturally relevant communications, expanded benchmarking requirements, and better access to energy and water data.
parent_statement: By 2030, achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings, reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings, and lower all natural gas-related emissions by 30%.
verbatim_text: Enhance resident and building owner understanding of energy savings opportunities, benefits, and climate impacts of energy consumption. This will be done through direct outreach, culturally relevant communications, expanding benchmarking requirements for all existing buildings, and better access to energy and water data.
mechanism_description: Requiring and improving access to building energy-use information increases awareness and transparency, which encourages efficiency actions and lower building energy consumption.

policy_statement: Achieve net-zero carbon buildings through new energy codes, amendments, incentives, education, and collaboration with utilities to decarbonize new and existing buildings.
parent_statement: By 2030, achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings, reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings, and lower all natural gas-related emissions by 30%.
verbatim_text: Achieve goal milestones for net-zero carbon buildings through new building energy codes, amendments, and other methods. Engage owners and operators of existing buildings to decarbonize through incentives and education for contractors and occupants. Collaborate with local utilities to implement equitable emission reduction strategies.
mechanism_description: New and amended building energy codes drive more efficient building design and operations, reducing energy use and associated emissions in new and existing buildings.

policy_statement: Prioritize local emissions reduction investment and create equitable workforce development and training opportunities for emerging building technologies through partnerships serving low-income communities and communities of color.
parent_statement: By 2030, achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings, reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings, and lower all natural gas-related emissions by 30%.
verbatim_text: Prioritize investment in local emissions reduction and create equitable workforce development and training opportunities for emerging technologies by partnering with local unions, education, and advocacy organizations that serve low-income communities and communities of color.
mechanism_description: Workforce training and equitable partnerships build local capacity to deploy emerging clean technologies, enabling broader emissions reduction investments and implementation.

policy_statement: By 2030, reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25%.
verbatim_text: By 2030, reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25%.
mechanism_description: Managing refrigerants to prevent leakage reduces high-global-warming-potential fugitive greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Develop a refrigerant destruction program that prices older high-ozone-depleting and high-global-warming-potential refrigerants to increase capture and safe destruction.
parent_statement: By 2030, reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25%.
verbatim_text: Develop a refrigerant destruction program that places a price on older high ozone-depleting substances and global warming potential (GWP) refrigerants. The program could be run by the City or a contractor, increasing the capture of old refrigerants and safely destroying harmful gases.
mechanism_description: A refrigerant destruction program uses pricing incentives to increase recovery and safe destruction of high-GWP refrigerants, reducing fugitive climate pollutant emissions.

policy_statement: Follow revised building code developments and move forward with local code amendments and actions to encourage low- and no-GWP refrigerants as soon as feasible.
parent_statement: By 2030, reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25%.
verbatim_text: Closely follow developments in revised building codes that allow the use of low and no GWP refrigerants, such as California's state building code changes and U.S. Green Building Council ® policies, and move forward with code amendments and other local action as soon as feasible.
mechanism_description: Updating local building codes to encourage low- and no-GWP refrigerants reduces climate pollution by replacing high-emitting refrigerants and lowering fugitive refrigerant emissions.

policy_statement: Partner with commercial refrigeration and HVAC stakeholders to create incentives for refrigerant leak detection, prevention, and repair.
parent_statement: By 2030, reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25%.
verbatim_text: Partner with grocery stores, convenience stores, restaurants, restaurant supply companies, refrigerated warehouses, and HVAC tune-up and repair companies to create an incentive for designing and tracking refrigerant leak detection, prevention, and repair.
mechanism_description: Incentivizing refrigerant leak detection, prevention, and repair reduces releases of high-impact refrigerant gases from commercial cooling and HVAC systems.

policy_statement: Create an awareness, education, and training campaign for HVAC providers and building stakeholders on refrigerant management and leak prevention, detection, and repair.
parent_statement: By 2030, reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25%.
verbatim_text: Create an awareness, education, and training campaign for local HVAC service providers, building owners, operators, inspectors, and maintenance leads on the importance of refrigerant management and strategies for leak detection, prevention, and repair.
mechanism_description: Training HVAC providers and building stakeholders on refrigerant leak prevention, detection, and repair improves refrigerant management practices and reduces high-global-warming-potential fugitive emissions.

policy_statement: Reduce and prevent refrigerant use to the extent possible, especially refrigerants with high global warming potential.
parent_statement: By 2030, reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25%.
verbatim_text: Emphasize the link between design and refrigerant use by reducing and preventing the use of refrigerants to the extent possible, particularly those with high GWP.
mechanism_description: Reducing and avoiding high-GWP refrigerants lowers fugitive refrigerant emissions and therefore decreases climate warming impacts.

policy_statement: By 2030, reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline.
verbatim_text:
By 2030, reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline*.

*The embodied carbon associated with the construction and materials must not exceed 500 kg CO2e/m 2 (~100 lbs. CO2e/sf) per project.
mechanism_description: Reducing the carbon intensity of building materials lowers emissions associated with construction and embodied lifecycle impacts.

policy_statement: Develop City of Austin design and construction specifications and purchasing agreements for healthy, low-carbon buildings in partnership with other cities and states.
parent_statement: By 2030, reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline.
verbatim_text: In partnership with other cities and states, develop City of Austin design and construction specifications and purchasing agreements to result in healthy, low-carbon buildings.
mechanism_description: Adopting low-carbon building specifications and purchasing standards reduces the embodied emissions associated with construction materials and building delivery.

policy_statement: Enhance and integrate lower-carbon building materials and deconstruction practices into city incentive programs and transition voluntary guidance into development agreements over time.
parent_statement: By 2030, reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline.
verbatim_text: Enhance and integrate lower-carbon building materials and deconstruction practices into City incentive programs, like the expedited permitting process and Austin Energy's Green Building program, to transition voluntary design guidance into planning and development agreements over time.
mechanism_description: Using incentives and development agreements to encourage lower-carbon materials and deconstruction practices reduces the embodied greenhouse gas emissions associated with building construction and demolition.

policy_statement: Create a performance framework and educational programming to reduce the lifecycle and health impacts of building materials and construction practices, focusing on low-income communities and communities of color.
parent_statement: By 2030, reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline.
verbatim_text: Create a performance framework and educational programming for industry professionals and the general public, focusing on low-income communities and communities of color, to reduce the lifecycle and negative health impacts of building materials and construction practices.
mechanism_description: A performance framework and education program steer builders and the public toward lower-impact construction materials and practices, reducing embodied emissions from construction.

policy_statement: Prioritize partnerships in local materials markets to decarbonize high-impact building materials such as glass, steel, aluminum, concrete, drywall, insulation, and carpet.
parent_statement: By 2030, reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline.
verbatim_text: Prioritize partnerships within local materials markets to decarbonize high-impact materials, specifically glass, steel, aluminum, concrete, drywall, insulation, and carpet.
mechanism_description: Decarbonizing the production and procurement of high-impact building materials reduces the embodied emissions associated with construction.

policy_statement: By 2030, equitably achieve a community-wide water demand of approximately 152,000 acre-feet per year by implementing Water Forward strategies.
verbatim_text: By 2030, equitably achieve a community-wide water demand of approximately 152,000 acre-feet per year by implementing strategies in the Water Forward Plan.
mechanism_description: Implementing water efficiency and conservation strategies reduces overall community water demand on a per-person basis.

policy_statement: Enhance community engagement and partnerships to advance equity through Water Forward strategies, including advanced metering, incentives, and ordinances.
parent_statement: By 2030, equitably achieve a community-wide water demand of approximately 152,000 acre-feet per year by implementing Water Forward strategies.
verbatim_text: Enhance community engagement strategies and create partnerships with community organizations to advance equity through Water Forward strategies, including My ATX Water (Advanced Metering Infrastructure), incentives, and ordinances.
mechanism_description: Advanced metering, incentives, and water ordinances help households and organizations use water more efficiently, reducing per-capita water demand.

policy_statement: Evaluate water conservation, customer assistance, and workforce development program criteria to address structural barriers to participation and expand enrollment for low-income customers.
parent_statement: By 2030, equitably achieve a community-wide water demand of approximately 152,000 acre-feet per year by implementing Water Forward strategies.
verbatim_text: Evaluate program criteria to identify opportunities to address structural barriers that prevent program participation. Collaborate with City departments and community organizations to explore strategies to expand enrollment in the Customer Assistance Program and increase participation in other programs serving low-income customers.
mechanism_description: The policy revises program rules and outreach to remove participation barriers so more low-income residents can enroll in climate-related assistance and conservation programs.

policy_statement: Assess links between water demand reduction and energy consumption, and pursue synergistic water conservation and energy management efforts through programs and partnerships.
parent_statement: By 2030, equitably achieve a community-wide water demand of approximately 152,000 acre-feet per year by implementing Water Forward strategies.
verbatim_text: Assess how water demand reduction is associated with energy consumption in residential and commercial buildings and Austin Water facilities. Identify and pursue synergistic water conservation and energy management optimization efforts through programs and partnerships.
mechanism_description: Coordinated water conservation and energy management efforts reduce water demand and the energy needed to move, treat, and use water across buildings and utility facilities.

policy_statement: By 2030, ensure 80% of new non-residential development is located within the city's activity centers and corridors.
verbatim_text: By 2030, 80% of new non-residential development is located within the city's Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors.
mechanism_description: Concentrating new non-residential development in activity centers and corridors reduces sprawl and supports lower-emission travel patterns through more compact, transit-supportive land use.

policy_statement: Adopt Complete Communities plans that reverse historical racist impacts, address displacement, and preserve neighborhoods in communities of color.
parent_statement: By 2030, ensure 80% of new non-residential development is located within the city's activity centers and corridors.
verbatim_text: Adopt Complete Communities plans that reverse historical and racist impacts on areas experiencing displacement and preserve neighborhoods in communities of color. Some examples include neighborhood, village, corridor, and station area plans.
mechanism_description: Adopting complete community and station-area plans promotes more connected, location-efficient neighborhood development that can reduce travel emissions while shaping lower-impact land-use patterns.

policy_statement: Work with medium and large employers to locate business sites in growth centers and along corridors.
parent_statement: By 2030, ensure 80% of new non-residential development is located within the city's activity centers and corridors.
verbatim_text: Work with medium and large employers to locate their places of business in growth centers and along corridors.
mechanism_description: Locating employers in growth centers and corridors concentrates jobs in transit-supportive areas, reducing car dependence and lowering transport emissions while supporting more efficient land use.

policy_statement: Create community mobility hubs adjacent to transit stops that offer first- and last-mile mobility options and a complete trip experience.
parent_statement: By 2030, ensure 80% of new non-residential development is located within the city's activity centers and corridors.
verbatim_text: Create community mobility hubs, including park-and-ride facilities, adjacent to transit stops to offer a complete trip experience. These hubs should offer various first- and last-mile mobility options.
mechanism_description: Mobility hubs improve first- and last-mile access to transit, making sustainable travel more convenient and shifting trips away from higher-emission private vehicle use.

policy_statement: Phase out free employee parking at city facilities and other large employers near the transit priority network and replace it with commuter benefit options.
parent_statement: By 2030, ensure 80% of new non-residential development is located within the city's activity centers and corridors.
verbatim_text: Phase out the practice of providing free parking spaces to employees at City of Austin facilities and other large employers located within 1/2 mile of the Austin Strategic Mobility Plan's transit priority network. Instead, offer a parking cash-out, carpool and vanpool options, flex schedules, transit passes, support for teleworking, or other commuter benefits programs.
mechanism_description: Eliminating free workplace parking and offering commuter alternatives shifts trips away from solo driving toward transit, carpooling, and reduced commuting, lowering transportation emissions.

policy_statement: By 2027, preserve and produce 135,000 housing units, including 60,000 affordable units, with 75% of new housing within half a mile of activity centers and corridors.
verbatim_text:
By 2027, preserve and produce 135,000 housing units, including 60,000 affordable housing units*, with 75% of new housing located within ½ mile of Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors.

*This goal is based on the Council-adopted Austin Strategic Housing Blueprint.
mechanism_description: Concentrating new housing near activity centers and corridors supports compact development and lower-carbon travel patterns, reducing transportation emissions and land use impacts.

policy_statement: Engage directly with communities vulnerable to displacement, connect them with services, and proactively monitor at-risk affordable housing properties to extend affordability.
parent_statement: By 2027, preserve and produce 135,000 housing units, including 60,000 affordable units, with 75% of new housing within half a mile of activity centers and corridors.
verbatim_text: Engage directly with communities that are vulnerable to displacement and connect them with services. Proactively monitor affordable housing properties at risk of losing their affordability status to extend the period of affordability.
mechanism_description: Direct outreach, service connection, and monitoring of at-risk affordable housing help preserve affordability and reduce displacement of vulnerable residents.

policy_statement: Increase funding for the city's Housing Trust Fund and support capacity building for community development corporations.
parent_statement: By 2027, preserve and produce 135,000 housing units, including 60,000 affordable units, with 75% of new housing within half a mile of activity centers and corridors.
verbatim_text: Increase funding for the City's current Housing Trust Fund and support capacity building for community development corporations.
mechanism_description: Increasing housing trust funding and building community development capacity strengthens institutions that can enable future climate-related housing and community interventions.

policy_statement: Include low-income communities, communities of color, and people with disabilities in housing review and anti-displacement program design, with culturally relevant outreach for subsidized units in gentrifying areas.
parent_statement: By 2027, preserve and produce 135,000 housing units, including 60,000 affordable units, with 75% of new housing within half a mile of activity centers and corridors.
verbatim_text: Include low-income communities, communities of color, and people with disabilities directly affected by systemic inequalities in the City's Housing Investment Review Committee activities and when creating anti-displacement programs. Enhance direct outreach of Housing and Planning Department-subsidized affordable units in gentrifying areas with culturally relevant communication strategies.
mechanism_description: Inclusive housing review, anti-displacement program design, and targeted outreach improve access to affordable housing and help reduce displacement pressures, strengthening community resilience.

policy_statement: By 2030, ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided through working from home.
verbatim_text:
By 2030, 50% of trips in Austin are made using public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided altogether by working from home*.

*Based on City of Austin and Capital Metro data, includes all trips, and was approximately 20% in 2019.
mechanism_description: Increasing the share of trips made by transit, active transportation, shared rides, or avoided travel reduces vehicle miles traveled and lowers transportation emissions.

policy_statement: Work with the Austin Transit Partnership to implement Project Connect and expand and improve public transportation services.
parent_statement: By 2030, ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided through working from home.
verbatim_text: Work with the Austin Transit Partnership to implement Project Connect and expand and improve public transportation services.
mechanism_description: Expanding and improving public transportation service enables more trips by transit instead of private cars, reducing transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Create comprehensive, user-friendly resources connecting community members with free transportation options, including expanded free transit access.
parent_statement: By 2030, ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided through working from home.
verbatim_text:
Create comprehensive, user-friendly resources connecting community members with free transportation options.

- Expand free transit options, including through Capital Metro, to provide increased transportation access for low-income communities, communities of color, and people with disabilities.
mechanism_description: Expanding free public transit access increases transit use in place of private vehicle travel, reducing transportation emissions.

policy_statement: Partner to improve transit stops, stations, and access to these facilities with engagement from low-income communities, communities of color, and people with disabilities.
parent_statement: By 2030, ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided through working from home.
verbatim_text: Partner with Capital Metro, developers, and community organizers to engage low-income communities, communities of color, and people with disabilities to improve transit stops, stations, and access to these facilities.
mechanism_description: Improving transit stops, stations, and access makes public transportation easier and more attractive to use, which can increase ridership and reduce transportation emissions.

policy_statement: Implement Proposition B transportation infrastructure for bikeways, sidewalks, trails, safety, safe routes to school, and substandard streets in low-income communities and communities of color through meaningful engagement.
parent_statement: By 2030, ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided through working from home.
verbatim_text: Implement the 2020 Proposition B voter-approved general obligation bonds for transportation infrastructure, including sidewalks, transportation-related bikeways, urban trails, transportation safety projects, safe routes to school, and substandard streets in low-income communities and communities of color through a meaningful community engagement process.
mechanism_description: Building bikeways, sidewalks, trails, and safe school routes makes walking and cycling more viable and safe, shifting travel away from cars and lowering transportation emissions.

policy_statement: Provide free or reduced-price bicycles and bicycle training for communities of color and train police officers on bicycle laws and racial profiling.
parent_statement: By 2030, ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided through working from home.
verbatim_text: Provide access to free or reduced-priced bicycles and basic bicycle training for communities of color and train police officers on bicycle laws and racial profiling to improve the comfort and safety of people of color riding bicycles.
mechanism_description: Providing affordable bicycles, rider training, and safer conditions for cyclists increases biking in place of higher-emission travel modes, reducing transport emissions.

policy_statement: Update the city's Sidewalk Plan and Urban Trails Plan with an emphasis on equity and meaningful community engagement.
parent_statement: By 2030, ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided through working from home.
verbatim_text: Update the City's Sidewalk Plan and Urban Trails Plan, emphasizing equity and meaningful community engagement.
mechanism_description: Updating sidewalk and urban trail plans helps enable walking and active transportation, leading to reduced reliance on higher-emission vehicle trips.

policy_statement: Actively promote remote work, telework, and flexible commute policies to maintain and further reduce single-occupancy vehicle trips.
parent_statement: By 2030, ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided through working from home.
verbatim_text: Actively promote remote work, telework, and flexible commute policies to maintain and encourage further reductions in single-occupancy vehicle trips.
mechanism_description: Promoting telework and flexible commute options reduces single-occupancy vehicle trips, lowering transportation-related greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Implement City of Austin telework policies aligned with transportation mode-shift goals, including encouraging no less than 85% of eligible City employees to participate in telework and contributing toward 15% of Austin residents working from home by 2039.
parent_statement: By 2030, ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided through working from home.
verbatim_text: Ensure that all policies for telework, remote work, or mandatory commuting that impact City of Austin employees are in full alignment with the goal of reducing single-occupancy vehicle trips in the region and that any future changes to City policies contribute towards reaching the 50% mode-shift goal by 2030, and whenever appropriate, all new City of Austin telework and remote work policies contribute towards the goal of achieving 15% Austin residents working from home by 2039. This should include a strategy to implement a City of Austin telework policy encouraging no less than 85% of eligible City of Austin employees to participate in telework.
mechanism_description: Telework policies reduce commute trips, especially single-occupancy vehicle travel, which lowers transportation-related greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: By 2030, electrify 40% of total vehicle miles traveled in Austin and ensure electric vehicle ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse.
verbatim_text: By 2030, 40% of total vehicle miles traveled in Austin are electrified, and electric vehicle ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse. This translates to approximately 460,000 electric vehicles on the road.
mechanism_description: Increasing the share of travel done by electric vehicles displaces gasoline and diesel vehicle use and lowers transportation emissions.

policy_statement: Complete an Electric Vehicle Community Needs Assessment to identify mobility, electrification, and justice needs and inform an EV adoption growth plan.
parent_statement: By 2030, electrify 40% of total vehicle miles traveled in Austin and ensure electric vehicle ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse.
verbatim_text: Complete an Electric Vehicle Community Needs Assessment to identify the intersections of mobility challenges, transportation electrification, and racial and economic justice. The assessment will inform an EV adoption growth plan that will be supported by enhanced communications efforts and incentives.
mechanism_description: A community assessment identifies mobility and equity needs to guide planning, outreach, and incentives that enable greater electric vehicle adoption.

policy_statement: Collaborate with community partners to create inclusive, accessible incentives for buying or leasing electric vehicles, prioritizing low-income communities, communities of color, and areas with limited transit access.
parent_statement: By 2030, electrify 40% of total vehicle miles traveled in Austin and ensure electric vehicle ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse.
verbatim_text: Collaborate with community partners to create inclusive and easily accessible incentives for buying or leasing electric vehicles. This strategy would supplement existing state and federal programs, prioritize low-income communities and communities of color, and focus on geographic areas with limited or no access to transit.
mechanism_description: Accessible purchase and lease incentives increase electric vehicle adoption, shifting travel away from fossil-fueled cars and reducing transportation emissions.

policy_statement: Partner with mobility authorities to offer reduced or eliminated toll rates for low-income communities and communities of color who drive EVs from the Eastern Crescent to central Austin.
parent_statement: By 2030, electrify 40% of total vehicle miles traveled in Austin and ensure electric vehicle ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse.
verbatim_text: Partner with highway and regional mobility authorities to offer a reduced or eliminated toll rate for low-income communities and communities of color who drive EVs on toll roads from the Eastern Crescent to central Austin.
mechanism_description: Reduced tolls for EV drivers lower the cost of electric vehicle use, encouraging EV adoption and supporting lower transportation emissions.

policy_statement: Create an electric bike and car-sharing program centered on low-income communities and communities of color to provide low-cost zero-emissions mobility.
parent_statement: By 2030, electrify 40% of total vehicle miles traveled in Austin and ensure electric vehicle ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse.
verbatim_text: Create an electric bike and car-sharing program centered on low-income communities and communities of color to support functional, low-cost, zero-emissions mobility.
mechanism_description: Expanding shared electric bikes and car-sharing increases access to low-emission travel options and reduces transportation emissions by displacing higher-emitting trips.

policy_statement: Encourage public sector fleets in the Austin-Round Rock-San Marcos region to commit to buying 100% electric vehicles when available, cost-competitive, and operationally suitable.
parent_statement: By 2030, electrify 40% of total vehicle miles traveled in Austin and ensure electric vehicle ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse.
verbatim_text: Encourage vehicle fleets from public sector agencies in the Austin-Round Rock-San Marcos MSA, such as cities, counties, Capital Metro, and school districts, to commit to buying 100% electric vehicles when they are available, cost-competitive, and meet operational needs.
mechanism_description: Public agencies shift fleet vehicle purchasing toward electric vehicles, which reduces fossil fuel use and associated transportation emissions.

policy_statement: By 2030, transition 100% of gig, rideshare, public health, and delivery vehicle fleets to electric, beginning with private fleets in the Eastern Crescent using pilots, support, regulation, incentives, and education.
parent_statement: By 2030, electrify 40% of total vehicle miles traveled in Austin and ensure electric vehicle ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse.
verbatim_text: By 2030, transition 100% of the gig, rideshare, public health, and delivery vehicle fleets to electric. Starting with private fleets in the Eastern Crescent, establish pilots, technical support, regulation, incentives, and education to support rapid electrification.
mechanism_description: Electrifying vehicle fleets replaces fossil-fuel vehicle use with electric driving, which lowers transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: By 2030, develop an equitably distributed mix of level 1, level 2, and DC fast charging infrastructure sufficient to accommodate 40% of total vehicle miles traveled in the city.
verbatim_text: By 2030, Austin has a compelling and equitably distributed mix of level 1, 2, and DC fast charging infrastructure to accommodate 40% of total vehicle miles traveled in the city. This translates to 226 megawatts of electrical load and could mean more than 37,000 charging ports.
mechanism_description: Expanding publicly available EV charging capacity makes electric vehicle use more feasible, enabling wider adoption and downstream transport emissions reductions.

policy_statement: Continue to incentivize EV charging installation by the city, businesses, manufacturers, and third parties to create a low-cost, reliable network accessible to all.
parent_statement: By 2030, develop an equitably distributed mix of level 1, level 2, and DC fast charging infrastructure sufficient to accommodate 40% of total vehicle miles traveled in the city.
verbatim_text: Continue to incentivize the installation of EV charging infrastructure by the City, businesses, auto manufacturers, and third-party charging companies to create a compelling (convenient, reliable, and low-cost) network accessible to all.
mechanism_description: Incentivizing the buildout of EV chargers creates a more accessible and reliable charging network, which supports wider electric vehicle adoption.

policy_statement: By 2030, build a network of intelligent charging that supports grid reliability, resilience, efficiency, lower emissions, accessibility, and lower costs, and incentivize internet-connected charging infrastructure.
parent_statement: By 2030, develop an equitably distributed mix of level 1, level 2, and DC fast charging infrastructure sufficient to accommodate 40% of total vehicle miles traveled in the city.
verbatim_text: By 2030, the City will have a network of intelligent charging that supports grid reliability and resilience, maximized efficiency, reduced emissions, accessibility for all, and lower costs for all residents. Incentivize internet-connected charging infrastructure with the ability to manage the start and end time and charge rate across 24 hours while still meeting the driver's needs.
mechanism_description: Incentivizing intelligent connected EV charging infrastructure makes electric vehicle use more practical and scalable, enabling lower-emission transport and better alignment with grid conditions.

policy_statement: Adopt new energy and building codes that address future EV charging needs and lower barriers to installing charging infrastructure more equitably.
parent_statement: By 2030, develop an equitably distributed mix of level 1, level 2, and DC fast charging infrastructure sufficient to accommodate 40% of total vehicle miles traveled in the city.
verbatim_text: Adopt new energy and building codes that address future EV charging needs and enable a more equitable approach by simplifying the charging network and lowering barriers to entry for installing EV charging.
mechanism_description: Updating building and energy codes to make EV charging easier to install expands charging infrastructure, which enables greater electric vehicle adoption.

policy_statement: Expand culturally competent outreach on EV charging incentives and installation to community groups, professional organizations, unions, and property managers.
parent_statement: By 2030, develop an equitably distributed mix of level 1, level 2, and DC fast charging infrastructure sufficient to accommodate 40% of total vehicle miles traveled in the city.
verbatim_text: Expand outreach to community groups, professional organizations, unions, and property managers using culturally competent information about EV charging incentives and installation. Collaborate with and learn from existing community and City partnerships.
mechanism_description: Targeted outreach about EV charging incentives and installation is intended to increase awareness and participation, leading to more charging access and enabling electric vehicle adoption.

policy_statement: Make the Austin-Round Rock-San Marcos area a leader in transportation electrification by adopting policies and technologies that maximize economic and health benefits while supporting industry growth.
verbatim_text: The Austin-Round Rock-San Marcos area is a leader in transportation electrification by adopting policies and technologies that maximize economic and health benefits while supporting the growth of this emerging industry.
mechanism_description: Electrifying transportation shifts vehicles away from fossil fuels, reducing greenhouse gas emissions from the transport sector.

policy_statement: The city will lead creation of a regional coalition of government, business, and community stakeholders to support EV adoption in the five-county metropolitan area.
parent_statement: Make the Austin-Round Rock-San Marcos area a leader in transportation electrification by adopting policies and technologies that maximize economic and health benefits while supporting industry growth.
verbatim_text: The City will take the lead in creating a regional coalition to support EV adoption within the five-county MSA. The coalition will consist of an inclusive group of government, business, and community stakeholders.
mechanism_description: Creating a regional stakeholder coalition is intended to coordinate action and support wider electric vehicle adoption, enabling lower transportation emissions over time.

policy_statement: Austin will continue to pilot and early-adopt emerging transportation electrification technologies and ensure low-income communities and communities of color access the benefits first.
parent_statement: Make the Austin-Round Rock-San Marcos area a leader in transportation electrification by adopting policies and technologies that maximize economic and health benefits while supporting industry growth.
verbatim_text: Austin will continue to pilot and be an early adopter of emerging technologies for transportation electrification and ensure that low-income communities and communities of color can access the benefits first.
mechanism_description: Deploying and early-adopting transportation electrification technologies shifts transport away from fossil fuels and reduces transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Austin will lead a just transition to an electrified regional transportation system by collaborating with workforce and community groups to position low-income communities and communities of color to benefit.
parent_statement: Make the Austin-Round Rock-San Marcos area a leader in transportation electrification by adopting policies and technologies that maximize economic and health benefits while supporting industry growth.
verbatim_text: Austin will be a leader in the just transition to an electrified regional transportation system by collaborating with community and workforce leadership groups like labor unions, grassroots organizations, and businesses. We will ensure that low-income communities and communities of color are positioned to benefit from the switch to electric transportation.
mechanism_description: Electrifying the transportation system reduces fossil fuel use in vehicles and lowers transport-related greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Create a robust electric transportation economic cluster in Central Texas by supporting new and existing local EV supply-chain companies.
parent_statement: Make the Austin-Round Rock-San Marcos area a leader in transportation electrification by adopting policies and technologies that maximize economic and health benefits while supporting industry growth.
verbatim_text: Create a robust electric transportation economic cluster in Central Texas by supporting economic development for new and existing local companies focused on the EV supply chain, including battery technology, vehicle manufacturing, and software.
mechanism_description: Supporting EV supply-chain industries increases the availability and maturity of electric vehicle technologies, which helps enable broader EV adoption and lower transport emissions.

policy_statement: By 2030, ensure all Austinites can access a community-driven food system that addresses food insecurity, prioritizes regenerative agriculture, supports dietary agency, promotes plant-based foods, and minimizes food waste.
verbatim_text: By 2030, ensure all Austinites can access a food system that is communitydriven, addresses food insecurity, prioritizes regenerative agriculture, supports dietary and health agency, promotes plant-based foods, and minimizes food waste.
mechanism_description: Promoting regenerative agriculture changes farming practices in ways that increase soil carbon storage and restore ecosystem functions.

policy_statement: Apply a purchasing framework, support supply-chain infrastructure, and build a regional food system network to increase institutional and corporate procurement of pro-climate, pro-health food options.
parent_statement: By 2030, ensure all Austinites can access a community-driven food system that addresses food insecurity, prioritizes regenerative agriculture, supports dietary agency, promotes plant-based foods, and minimizes food waste.
verbatim_text: Apply a purchasing framework, support supply-chain infrastructure, and build a regional food system network to bolster institutional and corporate food procurement of proclimate, pro-health options.
mechanism_description: Procurement rules and supporting food-system infrastructure increase purchases of climate-friendly food, which lowers upstream agricultural and food supply chain emissions.

policy_statement: Implement community-driven programs to incentivize and promote more affordable and culturally relevant pro-climate, pro-health prepared and retail food choices while minimizing displacement.
parent_statement: By 2030, ensure all Austinites can access a community-driven food system that addresses food insecurity, prioritizes regenerative agriculture, supports dietary agency, promotes plant-based foods, and minimizes food waste.
verbatim_text: Implement community-driven programs to incentivize and promote more affordable and culturally relevant pro-climate, pro-health choices in prepared and retail food options, focusing on minimizing displacement.
mechanism_description: Community-led incentives and promotion encourage people to choose lower-emissions prepared and retail foods, reducing emissions associated with food consumption.

policy_statement: Develop community-driven programs and tools to equitably engage and empower Austin communities to make affordable and culturally relevant pro-climate, pro-health food choices.
parent_statement: By 2030, ensure all Austinites can access a community-driven food system that addresses food insecurity, prioritizes regenerative agriculture, supports dietary agency, promotes plant-based foods, and minimizes food waste.
verbatim_text: Develop a variety of community-driven programs and tools to equitably engage and empower the full spectrum of Austin's communities to make affordable and culturally relevant pro-climate, prohealth food choices.
mechanism_description: Community-based engagement programs encourage residents to choose lower-emissions food options, reducing climate impacts from food consumption.

policy_statement: Conduct a food waste root cause analysis and implement changes to increase food waste reduction practices by 50% across single-family, multi-family, and commercial properties.
parent_statement: By 2030, ensure all Austinites can access a community-driven food system that addresses food insecurity, prioritizes regenerative agriculture, supports dietary agency, promotes plant-based foods, and minimizes food waste.
verbatim_text: Conduct a food waste root cause analysis and implement changes informed by the analysis to increase food waste reduction practices by 50%. These practices should support the U.S. EPA Food Recovery Hierarchy's highest and best-use model and include single-family, multi-family, and commercial properties.
mechanism_description: Identifying and changing the causes of food waste reduces the amount of organic waste sent for disposal, thereby avoiding landfill methane emissions.

policy_statement: By 2030, reduce greenhouse gas emissions from institutional, commercial, and government purchasing by at least 50%.
verbatim_text: By 2030, reduce greenhouse gas emissions from institutional, commercial, and government purchasing by at least 50%.
mechanism_description: Lowering the emissions intensity of institutional, commercial, and government purchasing reduces upstream supply-chain emissions associated with procured goods and services.

policy_statement: Develop a methodology to measure lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions and other impacts from non-residential purchasing and establish a baseline for tracking progress.
parent_statement: By 2030, reduce greenhouse gas emissions from institutional, commercial, and government purchasing by at least 50%.
verbatim_text: Develop a methodology to measure lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions and other environmental and social impacts from non-residential purchasing and identify a baseline for tracking progress.
mechanism_description: Developing a methodology and baseline for lifecycle emissions from purchasing creates an accounting system that enables tracking, oversight, and better-informed future emissions reduction actions.

policy_statement: Strengthen the City of Austin's Sustainable Procurement Program to serve as a local and national model.
parent_statement: By 2030, reduce greenhouse gas emissions from institutional, commercial, and government purchasing by at least 50%.
verbatim_text: Strengthen the City of Austin's Sustainable Procurement Program to serve as a model for others locally and nationally.
mechanism_description: A strengthened sustainable procurement program reduces emissions by requiring or encouraging the city to purchase lower-carbon goods and services across its supply chain.

policy_statement: Recruit at least 50% of local large institutional purchasers and at least 2,500 local organizations to adopt environmental and social sustainability procurement standards or guidelines.
parent_statement: By 2030, reduce greenhouse gas emissions from institutional, commercial, and government purchasing by at least 50%.
verbatim_text: Recruit at least 50% of local large institutional purchasers (2,500+ employees) and at least 2,500 local organizations of all sizes to collaboratively adopt a set of environmental and social sustainability procurement standards and/or guidelines.
mechanism_description: Adopting sustainability procurement standards shifts organizational purchasing toward lower-emission goods and services, reducing upstream supply-chain emissions across sectors.

policy_statement: Expand the City of Austin's Circular Economy Program to reduce consumption, support internal reuse and sharing, and support circular organizations and youth engagement.
parent_statement: By 2030, reduce greenhouse gas emissions from institutional, commercial, and government purchasing by at least 50%.
verbatim_text:
Expand the City of Austin's Circular Economy Program to:

- Support City departments in reducing consumption, for example, by:
- Reducing barriers to internal reuse of products and materials.
- Creating a system for sharing infrequently used items among departments.
mechanism_description: Expanding circular economy practices such as internal reuse and shared access to goods reduces demand for new materials, thereby lowering upstream production emissions and waste generation.

policy_statement: Aggressively pursue waste reduction, organics composting, and recycling to achieve the waste reduction goals in the 2023 Austin Resource Recovery Comprehensive Plan.
verbatim_text:
Aggressively pursue waste reduction, organics composting, and recycling to achieve the waste reduction goals in the 2023 Austin Resource Recovery Comprehensive Plan*.

*The plan calls for maintaining a community-wide per capita disposal rate of 4 pounds disposed per Austin Resource Recovery (ARR)-serviced household per day in the near term and working to achieve 1 pound disposed per ARR-serviced household per day over time.
mechanism_description: Reducing waste disposal by diverting materials through recycling, composting, and source reduction lowers the amount of waste sent to landfills and thereby avoids methane emissions.

policy_statement: Implement consumer awareness campaigns promoting reuse, repair, and the waste management hierarchy, including reuse challenges, Fix-It clinics, and the Austin Reuse Directory.
parent_statement: Aggressively pursue waste reduction, organics composting, and recycling to achieve the waste reduction goals in the 2023 Austin Resource Recovery Comprehensive Plan.
verbatim_text: Implement consumer awareness campaigns, such as community reuse challenges, promotion and expansion of Fix-It clinics and the Austin Reuse Directory, and educational campaigns that promote the community benefits of reuse and repair. Encourage campaigns that promote the waste management hierarchy, which places recycling as a last resort before disposal.
mechanism_description: Consumer education and outreach increase reuse and repair behaviors, reducing waste sent for disposal and the emissions associated with landfilling and material production.

policy_statement: Create equitably distributed neighborhood Eco-hubs that provide borrowing, reuse, and repair services.
parent_statement: Aggressively pursue waste reduction, organics composting, and recycling to achieve the waste reduction goals in the 2023 Austin Resource Recovery Comprehensive Plan.
verbatim_text: Create 'Eco-hubs' that provide equitably distributed in-person neighborhood centers for borrowing, reuse, and repair services.
mechanism_description: Providing borrowing, reuse, and repair services helps people use products longer and buy fewer new goods, reducing waste and the emissions associated with producing and disposing of materials.

policy_statement: Offer a workforce development program for repair and reuse skills, job placement, and entrepreneurship in local circular businesses, prioritizing low-income communities, youth, and communities of color.
parent_statement: Aggressively pursue waste reduction, organics composting, and recycling to achieve the waste reduction goals in the 2023 Austin Resource Recovery Comprehensive Plan.
verbatim_text: Offer a workforce development program that includes training for repair and reuse skills, job placement, and entrepreneurship in local circular businesses, such as those found in the Austin Circular Economy Storymap. Prioritize the needs and strengths of low-income communities, youth, and communities of color.
mechanism_description: Training workers and entrepreneurs in repair and reuse supports circular businesses that extend product lifetimes and reduce waste generation.

policy_statement: Offer financial incentives such as point-of-sale rebates or a sales tax holiday for products, repair services, and rentals with lower environmental and social impacts, including alternatives to single-use plastics.
parent_statement: Aggressively pursue waste reduction, organics composting, and recycling to achieve the waste reduction goals in the 2023 Austin Resource Recovery Comprehensive Plan.
verbatim_text: Offer financial incentives, such as point-of-sale rebates or a sales tax holiday, to encourage consumers to choose products, repair services, and rentals with lower negative environmental and social impact, including alternatives to single-use plastics.
mechanism_description: Financial incentives encourage consumers to buy, repair, or rent lower-impact and reusable goods, reducing demand for disposable products and the waste and upstream emissions associated with them.

policy_statement: Review and modify bulky item collection policies and programs so viable items are resold, repaired, or recycled.
parent_statement: Aggressively pursue waste reduction, organics composting, and recycling to achieve the waste reduction goals in the 2023 Austin Resource Recovery Comprehensive Plan.
verbatim_text: Review and modify policies and programs for the collection of bulky items that result in viable items being resold, repaired, or recycled.
mechanism_description: Changing bulky item collection programs to prioritize resale, repair, and recycling diverts waste from landfill and reduces associated methane emissions.

policy_statement: By 2030, legally protect an additional 20,000 acres of carbon pools on natural lands and manage all new and existing natural areas, about 70,000 acres total, for resilience.
verbatim_text: By 2030, legally protect an additional 20,000 acres of carbon pools on natural lands and manage all new and existing natural areas (approximately 70,000 acres total), focusing on resilience.
mechanism_description: Protecting and stewarding natural lands preserves existing carbon stocks and ecosystem functions while improving resilience.

policy_statement: Identify and legally protect additional woodland, grassland, and wetland systems, especially in eastern Travis County, prioritizing benefits for low-income communities and communities of color.
parent_statement: By 2030, legally protect an additional 20,000 acres of carbon pools on natural lands and manage all new and existing natural areas, about 70,000 acres total, for resilience.
verbatim_text: Identify additional woodland, grassland, and wetland systems for protection, focusing on new conservation lands in the Travis County Eastern Planning Areas while prioritizing benefits for lowincome communities and communities of color. Legally protect lands through mechanisms such as fee simple acquisition and conservation easements.
mechanism_description: Protecting existing woodlands, grasslands, and wetlands preserves ecosystems and avoids carbon losses from land conversion and degradation.

policy_statement: Prepare natural lands for climate change and avoid catastrophic carbon loss through active, intentional, and holistic management.
parent_statement: By 2030, legally protect an additional 20,000 acres of carbon pools on natural lands and manage all new and existing natural areas, about 70,000 acres total, for resilience.
verbatim_text: Prepare natural lands for climate change and avoid catastrophic loss of carbon pools through active, intentional, and holistic management.
mechanism_description: Active and holistic management of natural lands helps maintain ecosystem integrity and prevent the release of stored carbon from natural carbon pools.

policy_statement: Ensure natural lands are accessible to and positively perceived by the community so they provide greater community value and support for conservation and stewardship.
parent_statement: By 2030, legally protect an additional 20,000 acres of carbon pools on natural lands and manage all new and existing natural areas, about 70,000 acres total, for resilience.
verbatim_text: Ensure that natural lands are accessible to and perceived positively by the community. When more people use and feel a connection to natural areas, they provide more community value. In return, communities are more likely to support conservation and stewardship.
mechanism_description: Improving community access to and connection with natural lands increases support for conservation and stewardship, helping preserve ecosystems and stored carbon.

policy_statement: Protect the quantity and quality of source water for municipal supply and environmental flows against climate-driven threats such as heat, drought, flood, and wildfire.
parent_statement: By 2030, legally protect an additional 20,000 acres of carbon pools on natural lands and manage all new and existing natural areas, about 70,000 acres total, for resilience.
verbatim_text: Protect quantity and quality of source water for municipal supply and regional environmental flows - especially in the face of climate change-driven threats like heat, drought, flood, and wildfire.
mechanism_description: Protecting upstream water sources and their quality helps maintain reliable municipal water supply and environmental flows under climate-driven stresses.

policy_statement: By 2030, protect 500,000 acres of farmland from development in the five-county region through legal protections or regenerative agriculture programs.
verbatim_text: By 2030, protect 500,000 acres of farmland from development in the fivecounty region* through legal protections or regenerative agriculture programs.
mechanism_description: Protecting farmland from development preserves land-based carbon stocks and ecosystem functions by avoiding conversion to more emissions-intensive uses.

policy_statement: Identify prime and locally significant farmlands in the five-county region and protect them from development through bonds, land trusts, conservation easements, and similar methods.
parent_statement: By 2030, protect 500,000 acres of farmland from development in the five-county region through legal protections or regenerative agriculture programs.
verbatim_text: Identify lands with prime farmland soils and farmlands of unique and local significance as defined by the Sustainable SITES Initiative in the 5-county region and protect them from development. This can be done through land conservation bonds, agricultural land trusts such as the American Farmland Trust, Natural Resource Conservation Service and Trust for Public Lands programs, Travis County Conservation Easements, and similar methods.
mechanism_description: Protecting prime farmland from development preserves land-based carbon stocks, ecosystem functions, and agricultural landscapes that would otherwise be lost through conversion.

policy_statement: Address local and federal agricultural tax appraisal and exemption issues that contribute to desertification and soil loss.
parent_statement: By 2030, protect 500,000 acres of farmland from development in the five-county region through legal protections or regenerative agriculture programs.
verbatim_text: Address issues with local and federal agricultural tax appraisals and exemptions that contribute to desertification and soil loss.
mechanism_description: Reforming agricultural tax appraisal and exemption incentives changes land management behavior in ways that reduce desertification and soil degradation.

policy_statement: Support farmers in the five-county region, especially farmers of color, with direct financial assistance to implement carbon-related soil programs or regenerative agriculture practices.
parent_statement: By 2030, protect 500,000 acres of farmland from development in the five-county region through legal protections or regenerative agriculture programs.
verbatim_text: Support farmers in the 5-county region who want to implement carbon-related soil programs or regenerative agricultural practices by providing direct financial assistance, specifically for farmers of color.
mechanism_description: Financial support enables farmers to adopt regenerative agricultural practices that increase soil carbon storage and restore ecosystem health.

policy_statement: Support farmers starting regenerative agriculture by providing access to land and other resources, prioritizing farmers of color.
parent_statement: By 2030, protect 500,000 acres of farmland from development in the five-county region through legal protections or regenerative agriculture programs.
verbatim_text: Support farmers who want to start regenerative agricultural practices by providing access to land and other necessary resources, specifically prioritizing farmers of color.
mechanism_description: Providing farmers with land and resources enables regenerative agriculture practices that increase soil carbon sequestration and restore ecosystem health.

policy_statement: Expand the use of compost generated by local composting programs.
parent_statement: By 2030, protect 500,000 acres of farmland from development in the five-county region through legal protections or regenerative agriculture programs.
verbatim_text: Expand the use of compost generated by local composting programs.
mechanism_description: Expanding the use of locally produced compost supports organics composting systems that keep biodegradable waste out of landfills, reducing methane emissions while returning nutrients to soils.

policy_statement: Create and support agriculture-specific job creation and youth programs to drive a generational shift in farming, especially for aspiring farmers of color.
parent_statement: By 2030, protect 500,000 acres of farmland from development in the five-county region through legal protections or regenerative agriculture programs.
verbatim_text: Encourage a fundamental change in the next generation of farmers by creating and supporting agriculture-specific jobs creation programs and working lands-specific youth programs - especially for aspiring farmers of color.
mechanism_description: Agriculture-focused jobs and youth programs build the next generation of farmers, strengthening long-term capacity to manage and sustain working lands.

policy_statement: Achieve at least 50% citywide tree canopy cover by 2050, with an equitable focus on increasing canopy cover.
verbatim_text: Achieve at least 50% citywide tree canopy cover by 2050, focusing on increasing canopy cover equitably.
mechanism_description: Expanding urban tree canopy reduces urban heat and increases carbon sequestration through additional vegetation cover.

policy_statement: Adopt a no net loss policy for tree canopy on public lands.
parent_statement: Achieve at least 50% citywide tree canopy cover by 2050, with an equitable focus on increasing canopy cover.
verbatim_text: Adopt a 'no net loss' policy for tree canopy on public lands.
mechanism_description: A no-net-loss tree canopy policy preserves existing public-land vegetation, maintaining carbon storage, ecosystem functions, and urban cooling benefits.

policy_statement: Promote no net loss of tree canopy on private developments through increased tree protections and landscape regulation or developer and homeowners association incentives.
parent_statement: Achieve at least 50% citywide tree canopy cover by 2050, with an equitable focus on increasing canopy cover.
verbatim_text: Promote no net loss of tree canopy on private developments by increasing tree protections and landscape regulation or working with developers and homeowner's associations to create incentives.
mechanism_description: Protecting and maintaining tree canopy in private developments reduces urban heat and preserves carbon sequestration benefits.

policy_statement: Increase city funding for community tree-planting programs focused on low-income communities and communities of color.
parent_statement: Achieve at least 50% citywide tree canopy cover by 2050, with an equitable focus on increasing canopy cover.
verbatim_text: Increase City funding for community tree-planting programs focused on low-income communities and communities of color.
mechanism_description: Funding community tree planting expands urban canopy cover, which reduces urban heat and increases carbon sequestration.

policy_statement: Create a tree, water, and soil management and resilience guide and provide city technical and financial assistance for tree planting and care for residents and small businesses in low-income communities and communities of color.
parent_statement: Achieve at least 50% citywide tree canopy cover by 2050, with an equitable focus on increasing canopy cover.
verbatim_text: Create a tree, water and soil management, and resilience guide for various types of private property. Provide City technical and financial assistance for tree planting and care for residents and small businesses in low-income communities and communities of color to ensure long-term health and tree canopy benefits in Austin neighborhoods.
mechanism_description: Providing guidance and support for planting and maintaining trees on private property expands urban canopy, which reduces heat impacts and increases carbon sequestration.

policy_statement: By 2030, include all city-owned lands under a management plan that results in neutral or negative carbon emissions and maximizes community co-benefits.
verbatim_text: By 2030, include all City-owned lands under a management plan that results in neutral or negative carbon emissions and maximizes community co-benefits.
mechanism_description: Managing public lands to maintain or improve their carbon balance preserves ecosystems and avoids or reduces land-based emissions while delivering community co-benefits.

policy_statement: Prioritize carbon neutrality and community benefits in public land acquisition and management practices.
parent_statement: By 2030, include all city-owned lands under a management plan that results in neutral or negative carbon emissions and maximizes community co-benefits.
verbatim_text: Prioritize carbon neutrality and community benefits in land acquisition and management practices for public lands.
mechanism_description: Using carbon-focused criteria in public land acquisition and management helps protect and steward land in ways that preserve ecosystem functions and carbon storage.

policy_statement: Identify and reclaim mono-use, underused, and unconventional public spaces to increase community access and ecological function, with a focus on green infrastructure.
parent_statement: By 2030, include all city-owned lands under a management plan that results in neutral or negative carbon emissions and maximizes community co-benefits.
verbatim_text: Identify and reclaim mono-use, underused, and unconventional public spaces to increase community access and ecological function - such as utility easements, road rights-of-way, stormwater wet ponds, and cemeteries - with a focus on green infrastructure.
mechanism_description: Reclaiming underused public spaces for green infrastructure increases vegetated and multifunctional public land, improving stormwater absorption and reducing urban heat impacts.

policy_statement: Promote community stewardship and management of neighborhood public lands to ensure higher levels of care and maintenance.
parent_statement: By 2030, include all city-owned lands under a management plan that results in neutral or negative carbon emissions and maximizes community co-benefits.
verbatim_text: Promote community stewardship and management of neighborhood public lands to ensure higher levels of care and maintenance.
mechanism_description: Community stewardship of neighborhood public lands improves their maintenance and condition, helping sustain urban green space resilience and ecosystem benefits.

policy_statement: Explore using low-carbon and carbon farming practices in food forests and community gardens on public lands to improve sequestration, plant growth, fertilizer use, and soil water retention.
parent_statement: By 2030, include all city-owned lands under a management plan that results in neutral or negative carbon emissions and maximizes community co-benefits.
verbatim_text: Explore the ability of food forests and community gardens on public lands to use low-carbon and carbon farming practices. This agricultural practice can improve carbon sequestration rates in agricultural systems, aid in plant growth, reduce fertilizer use, and improve soil water retention.
mechanism_description: Using carbon farming practices in community food-growing areas increases soil carbon storage and improves ecosystem health through better soil moisture retention and reduced fertilizer demand.

policy_statement: Austin will achieve net-zero community-wide greenhouse gas emissions by 2040.
verbatim_text: Austin will achieve net-zero community-wide greenhouse gas emissions by 2040.
mechanism_description: A community-wide net-zero target sets an economy-wide pathway to drive reductions in greenhouse gas emissions across sectors.

policy_statement: Dakar has set a target to reduce GHG emissions by 54% by 2050.
verbatim_text:
Overall, the goals that the city could achieve are:

-    a 54% reduction in GHG emissions by 2050;
mechanism_description: A citywide greenhouse gas reduction target creates an overall pathway for cutting emissions across sectors.

policy_statement: Dakar has set a target to reduce the energy bill in its built heritage by 75% by 2050.
verbatim_text:
Overall, the goals that the city could achieve are:

- a 75% reduction in the energy bill in the built heritage of the City of Dakar by 2050;

policy_statement: Dakar aims to increase the share of LEDs to 75% of total street lighting lamps by 2050.
verbatim_text:
- Efficiency in public lighting
- Increase the share of LEDs to 75% of total street lighting lamps by 2050.

policy_statement: Dakar aims to increase the modal share of BRT to 20% and TER to 15% by 2050.
verbatim_text:
The reduction targets for each of the major sources  of  transportation  emissions  and urban resilience due to the strengthening of the TOD are:

- increase the modal shares of the BRT to 20% and the TER to 15% by 2050;
mechanism_description: Increasing the share of trips made by public transit shifts travel away from higher-emitting modes and lowers transportation emissions.

policy_statement: Dakar aims to increase the share of new households in transit-oriented development to 25% by 2050.

policy_statement: Dakar aims for 50% of organic waste and 30% of garden waste to be composted by 2050.

The targets for each of the waste sector's main sources of emissions are:

- Composting

mechanism_description: Composting organic and garden waste diverts biodegradable material from landfills, reducing methane emissions and producing useful compost that returns nutrients to soils.

policy_statement: Dakar aims for 32% of wastewater treatment to be done through activated sludge treatment by 2050.

- Wastewater treatment
-  32%  of  wastewater  treatment  by  2050 will  be  done  through  the  treatment  of activated sludge.

Climate change stressors are expected to affect the marine and fisheries sector: The potential  risks  threaten  coral  reef  communities  and  other  aquatic  communities. ... Thus, controlling these non-climate change stressors is a mandatory adaptation option to increase the resilience of this sector to climate change.
mechanism_description: Developing adaptation plans for the fisheries and marine sector strengthens institutional capacity to manage stressors and improve the climate resilience of marine ecosystems and fisheries.

5.3.2 Adaptation Plans to enhance the resilience of the Water Resources

Water  resources  sector  relies  on  three  water  resources:  desalinated  water,  brackish groundwater and treated wastewater to fulfill its water demand in domestic, agriculture, and industrial sectors. ... Thus, enhancing the resilience of systems for water supply and  management  is  a  mandatory  adaptation  step  to  minimize  the  impact  of  climate change  on  this  sector.
mechanism_description: The policy creates a water-sector adaptation planning framework that guides actions to strengthen the resilience of water supply and management systems to climate impacts.

verbatim_text:
5.3.3 Adaptation Plans to enhance the resilience of the Coastal Zone

Inundation of low lying areas due to SLR will have a great influence on human settlements and terrestrial and aquatic ecosystems of the coastal zone. ... Designing  adaptive  long-term  coastal  plans  to  restrict  establishing  major projects on vulnerable coasts is very urgent action that must be taken.
mechanism_description: Restricting major development in vulnerable coastal zones reduces exposure of people, infrastructure, and ecosystems to sea-level-rise flooding risks.

policy_statement: Develop vulnerability and risk assessment reports for coastal zones.
parent_statement: Adaptation Plans to enhance the resilience of the Coastal Zone set out short-, medium-, and long-term actions to address sea-level rise risks in Kuwait's coastal areas.
verbatim_text: Short-term actions                | ▪ Develop vulnerability and risk assessment reports
mechanism_description: Preparing coastal vulnerability and risk assessments generates decision-useful information that supports climate adaptation planning and risk reduction.

policy_statement: Provide open access information and communication networks for responsible coastal parties and sectors.
parent_statement: Adaptation Plans to enhance the resilience of the Coastal Zone set out short-, medium-, and long-term actions to address sea-level rise risks in Kuwait's coastal areas.
verbatim_text: ▪ Provide open access for information and communication networks for parties and sectors responsible
mechanism_description: Open-access information and communication networks improve data sharing and coordination among coastal stakeholders, supporting better climate adaptation planning and response.

policy_statement: Build capacity of government and non-government stakeholders for deployment of coastal adaptation activities.
parent_statement: Adaptation Plans to enhance the resilience of the Coastal Zone set out short-, medium-, and long-term actions to address sea-level rise risks in Kuwait's coastal areas.
verbatim_text: ▪ Capacity-building for government and non- government stakeholders on the deployment of adaptation activities.
mechanism_description: Building the skills and capabilities of public and private stakeholders enables more effective planning and implementation of coastal climate adaptation actions.

policy_statement: Increase decision-maker awareness of sea-level rise risks and damage to nearby urban areas.

verbatim_text: ▪ Increase decision-makers awareness of the risks of SLR and the damages to nearby urban areas.
mechanism_description: Improving decision-maker awareness of sea-level rise risks supports better adaptation planning and risk-informed urban decision-making.

policy_statement: Protect coastlines from sea-level rise by constructing protection barriers.

policy_statement: Require future high-risk coastal construction projects to be designed to withstand future sea-level rise scenarios.

policy_statement: Adaptation Plans to enhance the resilience of the Human Health Sector set out short-, medium-, and long-term actions to build the health sector’s capacity for increased climate-related health risks.
verbatim_text: Enhancing the building capacity of the health sector to be prepared for increased risks due to climate change must be set as an objective of climate change adaptation for this sector. Immediate adaptation actions should be taken to  ensure the achievement of this objective. These adaptation actions include but not limited to initiating researches to investigate the risks of climate change induced diseases, adjusting the office working hours to avoid working during maximum temperature in the day,  raising  the  awareness  of  how  to  respond  to  dust  and  heat  waves  events  and establishing national health alerts for dust and heat wave events.
mechanism_description: A structured adaptation plan builds the health sector’s preparedness and response capacity for climate-related hazards such as heat waves, dust events, and climate-sensitive diseases.

policy_statement: Increase public awareness of climate change effects and impacts on general health.

mechanism_description: Public education about climate change’s health impacts increases awareness, which can support more climate-resilient behavior and preparedness.

mechanism_description: Using media and public messaging increases awareness of climate-related health risks, which supports better public preparedness and adaptive response.

policy_statement: Increase drought-proof vegetation to reduce dust fallout from dust storms.

policy_statement: Include information and instructions about climate change effects on health in the early education curriculum.
parent_statement: Adaptation Plans to enhance the resilience of the Human Health Sector set out short-, medium-, and long-term actions to build the health sector’s capacity for increased climate-related health risks.
