policy_statement: Kuwait is implementing green belt and protected area expansion projects, including the Kuwait Green Wall Project, to reduce dust storm impacts by planting trees and shrubs along the border.
verbatim_text: Kuwait has undertaken some adaptation projects such as implementing green belts project and increasing the protected areas to adapt to a dust storm. The green belt projects consist of planted areas with trees and shrubs of 50-180 Km in length areas with trees and shrubs of 50-180 Km in length and 5-20 Km in width. These projects are the 5-year duration and started in 2015 (KUNA, 2015). Meanwhile, Kuwait has implemented a project called 'Kuwait Green Wall Project. This project aims to increase the protected areas by planting 315,000 trees for 420 Km wall along the borderline to hold back the moving sand.
mechanism_description: Planting trees and shrubs in green belts and protected areas stabilizes land and reduces blowing sand and dust storm impacts.

policy_statement: P-3AC establishes nine lines of action to reduce significant climate impacts and vulnerabilities in Portugal.
verbatim_text:
The Adaptation to Climate Change Action Programme - P-3AC (NAP) complements and systematises the work done in the context of the NAS, aiming at its second objective, the implementation of adaptation measures. NAP elects eight direct intervention lines in the territory and infrastructures, complemented by an additional line of action of transversal nature, seeking to respond to Portugal's significant impacts and vulnerabilities.

The nine lines of action established aim to reduce the significant impacts and vulnerabilities of the territory, namely:

- a) increased frequency and intensity of rural fires.
- b) increased frequency and intensity of heat waves.
- c) increased frequency and intensity of periods of drought and water scarcity.
- d) increased susceptibility to desertification.
- e) increased maximum temperature.
- f) increased frequency and intensity of extreme precipitation events.
- g) sea-level rise.
- h) increased frequency and intensity of extreme phenomena causing coastal overtopping and erosion.
mechanism_description: A national adaptation action program builds institutional capacity to plan and implement measures that reduce vulnerability to climate hazards across multiple sectors.

policy_statement: Under P-3AC, Portugal will carry out prevention of rural fires through structuring interventions in agricultural and forestry areas.
parent_statement: P-3AC establishes nine lines of action to reduce significant climate impacts and vulnerabilities in Portugal.
verbatim_text:
This Programme complements and systematizes the work carried out in the context of the ENAAC 2020 with a view to the second objective - to implement adaptation measures, grouped into 8 concrete lines of action of direct intervention in the territory and infrastructures, complemented by a transversal line of action) which aim to respond to the significant impacts and vulnerabilities identified for Portugal (Table 3):

1. Prevention of rural fires - structuring interventions in agricultural and forestry areas.
mechanism_description: Structured management interventions in agricultural and forest areas reduce the likelihood and severity of rural fires, lowering climate-related wildfire risk and damage.

policy_statement: Portugal approved the Carbon Neutrality Roadmap 2050 (RNC2050) as its long-term mitigation strategy and basis for the national carbon neutrality goal in 2050.
verbatim_text:
The Portuguese long-term vision for mitigation has been set in the Carbon Neutrality Roadmap 2050 (RNC2050), approved by the Council of Ministers nº. 107/2019, of 1 st of July, which constituted the Portuguese Long-Term Strategy submitted to the European Commission and to United Nations Framework Convention for Climate Change (UNFCCC).

It shall be stressed that the Portuguese carbon neutrality goal in 2050 has been established in the Resolution of the Council of Ministers n.º 107/2019, which as above mentioned it also approved the Carbon Neutrality Roadmap 2050 (RNC2050) that constituted the Portuguese Long-Term Strategy submitted to the European Commission and to United Nations Framework Convention for Climate Change (UNFCCC).
mechanism_description: A national carbon neutrality roadmap sets an economy-wide target that guides planning, investment, and policy toward long-term emissions reduction.

policy_statement: Portugal set P-3AC result targets including 100% municipal adaptation-plan coverage by 2030 and sectoral contingency/adaptation-plan targets across fire defence, water, transport, energy, wastewater, telecommunications, coastline erosion, invasive species, and vector-borne disease reduction.
verbatim_text:
Table 1. Result indicators and targets defined under the NAP (Adaptation to Climate Change Action Programme - P-3AC).

| Result indicator | Target 2020 56 | Target 2030 |
| Municipalities covered by adaptation plans (municipal, inter-municipal or regional) | 60% | 100% |
| Municipalities with forest fire defence plans that integrate future climate vulnerability assessments and adaptation measures | 2% | 100% |
| Water efficiency in urban consumption | 80% | 85% |
| Water efficiency in industrial consumption | 85% | 90% |
| Water use efficiency in agriculture | 65% | 80% |
| Target population of awareness or dissemination campaigns related to climate change impacts and adaptation measures | 5% | 25% |
| Transport infrastructure managing entities that have adaptation plans or contingency plans for extreme events | 10% | 50% |
| Energy production, transmission and distribution companies that have adaptation plans or contingency plans for extreme events | 25% | 100% |
mechanism_description: Establishing and expanding adaptation and contingency plans across municipalities and key sectors increases institutional preparedness and capacity to manage climate risks and extreme events.

policy_statement: Portugal committed as an EU Member State to mobilise 0.15% to 0.20% of GNI as ODA for Least Developed Countries by 2030.
verbatim_text: As an EU Member State, Portugal made the commitment to mobilise 0.15% to 0.20% of its GNI as ODA allocated to Least Developed Countries (LDC) by 2030.
mechanism_description: Providing development finance to least developed countries builds institutional, economic, and implementation capacity that can support climate mitigation and adaptation actions.

policy_statement: The City of Geneva commits to reducing greenhouse gas emissions by 60% by 2030 and achieving carbon neutrality by 2050.
verbatim_text: The City of Geneva declared a climate emergency in February 2020. It intends to reduce greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions by 60% by 2030 and to achieve carbon neutrality by 2050, in order to limit climate change and comply with the Paris Agreement.
mechanism_description: An economy-wide greenhouse gas reduction and carbon neutrality target creates an overarching pathway for reducing emissions across multiple sectors.
